message: fix michigan county archiving which was causing double counting of some rows

info.long_name: Cumulative reported cyclosporiasis cases since outbreak start, by Michigan county
info.short_description: Total cyclosporiasis cases reported in a Michigan county since the 2026 outbreak began (June 22, 2026), as of the most recent weekly update.
info.long_description: Cumulative count of laboratory-confirmed and probable cyclosporiasis cases reported to the Michigan Department of Health and Human Services (MDHHS) during the 2026 Southeast Michigan cyclosporiasis outbreak, broken out by county. MDHHS explicitly states these are cumulative case counts 'reported during the outbreak investigation period' (i.e. since June 22, 2026), NOT new cases in the past week - the value only grows or stays flat over time and should not be interpreted as a weekly incidence rate. MDHHS updates the county breakdown weekly, on Thursdays (data reported through 9:30am Eastern that day); a same-week re-run of this ingest will not duplicate a data point. 'Detroit City' is reported by MDHHS as its own jurisdiction (Detroit has an independent local health department) but is geographically part of Wayne County; its case counts have been summed into the Wayne County (FIPS 26163) total here so that every row represents a single county-level FIPS geography. Because the source page itself only ever displays the single latest cumulative snapshot (no historical table), this ingest script accumulates one dated snapshot per run into raw/mi_cyclo_county_snapshots.csv, building a real weekly time series across successive scheduled runs. The very first observed week for a given county will therefore only have one data point.
info.statement: In {location}, {value} cumulative cyclosporiasis cases had been reported as of {time}.

info.long_name: Newly reported cyclosporiasis cases since the prior weekly update, by Michigan county
info.short_description: Incident (newly reported) cyclosporiasis cases in a Michigan county since the previous weekly MDHHS update.
info.long_description: Derived measure: the week-over-week difference in mi_cyclo_cases_cumulative for the same county, i.e. the number of additional cases attributed to that county between two consecutive weekly MDHHS updates (Thursdays). Because MDHHS only publishes a single cumulative snapshot at a time (no historical archive on the source page), this column can only be computed once at least two weekly snapshots for a county have been collected by this ingest script; the first week a county appears will have a missing (NA) value here since there is no prior snapshot to difference against. Values should be non-negative in principle (cumulative counts should not decrease), but MDHHS notes that 'case counts...may change as additional information becomes available' (e.g. reclassification or removal of a case), so small negative values are possible and reflect genuine data revisions by the source, not a computation error.
info.statement: In {location}, {value} new cyclosporiasis cases were reported in the week ending {time}.

The very first observed week for a given county will therefore only have one data point.
mi_cyclo_cases_cumulative.statement: In {location}, {value} cumulative cyclosporiasis cases had been reported as of {time}.

mi_cyclo_cases_new.long_name: Newly reported cyclosporiasis cases since the prior weekly update, by Michigan county
mi_cyclo_cases_new.short_description: Incident (newly reported) cyclosporiasis cases in a Michigan county since the previous weekly MDHHS update.
mi_cyclo_cases_new.long_description: Derived measure: the week-over-week difference in mi_cyclo_cases_cumulative for the same county, i.e. the number of additional cases attributed to that county between two consecutive weekly MDHHS updates (Thursdays). Because MDHHS only publishes a single cumulative snapshot at a time (no historical archive on the source page), this column can only be computed once at least two weekly snapshots for a county have been collected by this ingest script; the first week a county appears will have a missing (NA) value here since there is no prior snapshot to difference against. mi_cyclo_cases_new.statement: In {location}, {value} new cyclosporiasis cases were reported in the week ending {time}.

mdhhs_cyclosporiasis_2026.name: MDHHS Cyclosporiasis Outbreak - Cases by County
mdhhs_cyclosporiasis_2026.organization: Michigan Department of Health and Human Services (MDHHS)
mdhhs_cyclosporiasis_2026.location: "Detailed Outbreak Data" accordion, "Cases by county" section, on the Infectious Disease Outbreaks page
mdhhs_cyclosporiasis_2026.description: In June-July 2026, Michigan experienced a large, ongoing multi-county cyclosporiasis outbreak (typical baseline is ~50 cases/year statewide; over 1,200 cases had been reported across 40+ counties by early July 2026), with Southeast Michigan (Monroe, Wayne, Washtenaw, Lenawee, etc.) most affected. The likely food source had not been identified as of this writing. MDHHS publishes a 'Cases by county' HTML table on its Infectious Disease Outbreaks page (a server-rendered Sitecore CMS page, not an interactive Power BI/ArcGIS/Tableau dashboard), updated weekly on Thursdays with data reported through 9:30am Eastern; the statewide daily total (shown elsewhere, e.g. in MDHHS press releases) is updated more frequently and is NOT the same number as the sum of this weekly county table. This is an active-outbreak-specific webpage and content/structure may change substantially, be consolidated into a different page, or be removed entirely once the outbreak is declared over - a future maintainer should verify the page still exists and still contains a 'Cases by county' table matching this ingest script's assumptions (see the extensive scraper comments in ingest.R) before assuming failures are a code bug.
mdhhs_cyclosporiasis_2026.restrictions: Public domain. State of Michigan government data is generally not subject to copyright restrictions; MDHHS content is publicly published for outbreak awareness.
